summary: Read-only audit confirmed a live confidence-gate regression. Runtime log still reports confidence_too_low rejections at threshold 0.600, but three live trades were opened below that floor (ids 241, 243, 244 with confidences 0.3863, 0.3279, 0.3769). This means the sub-0.60 entries are not explained by configured threshold drift and instead indicate a live path inconsistency between gate decisions and executed opens.
verification_evidence: .env and runtime banner in bot log still show min_conf=0.25 at startup, while active gate reject lines explicitly use confidence threshold 0.600.
bot log contains BRIDGE GATE REJECT lines using confidence_too_low:*:0.600 for other markets in the same runtime window.
bot log contains BRIDGE GATE PASS / open events for id=241 confidence=0.3863, id=243 confidence=0.3279, id=244 confidence=0.3769.
Trade DB rows for ids 241, 243, 244 confirm those opens exist with low confidence values.

For id=244 specifically, the same slug shows BRIDGE GATE PASS in core.model_bridge followed immediately by bot_core BRIDGE GATE REJECT confidence_too_low:0.3769:0.600 and then a trade open, proving contradictory live behavior in the same event flow.
residual_risks: This audit did not widen into a fix, so the root implementation bug remains active until a remediation task lands.
The evidence strongly suggests a double-path or ordering inconsistency between model_bridge pass logging, bot_core gate rejection, and actual open execution.
